title: Link
description: Animated link components with underline hover effects and customizable icons.
---

import { LinkDemo } from "@/registry/new-york/link-demo";

<ComponentPreview name="link">
  <LinkDemo />
</ComponentPreview>

## Installation

If you've already added the prsui registry, use the short command:

<CodeBlockCommand
  __bun__="bunx --bun shadcn@latest add @prsui/link"
  __npm__="npx shadcn@latest add @prsui/link"
  __pnpm__="pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @prsui/link"
  __yarn__="yarn shadcn@latest add @prsui/link"
/>

Otherwise, use the full registry URL:

<CodeBlockCommand
  __bun__="bunx --bun shadcn@latest add https://prsui.pr5.dev/r/link.json"
  __npm__="npx shadcn@latest add https://prsui.pr5.dev/r/link.json"
  __pnpm__="pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add https://prsui.pr5.dev/r/link.json"
  __yarn__="yarn shadcn@latest add https://prsui.pr5.dev/r/link.json"
/>

## Usage

After you add the component with the CLI, import it from your UI folder:

```tsx
import { Link01, Link02, Link03, Link04, Link05 } from "@/components/ui/link";

export function MyComponent() {
  return <Link01 href="https://example.com">Visit example</Link01>;
}
```

## Variants

| Variant  | Description                                                                  |
| -------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `Link01` | Underline animation from left to right on hover. External `<a>` tag.         |
| `Link02` | Underline animation from right to left on hover. External `<a>` tag.         |
| `Link03` | Underline animation from center outward on hover. External `<a>` tag.        |
| `Link04` | Full-width highlight with mix-blend-difference on hover. External `<a>` tag. |
| `Link05` | Left-to-right wipe with mix-blend-difference on hover. External `<a>` tag.   |

## Props

All variants extend the native `<a>` attributes via the shared `LinkProps` type, plus two extras:

| Prop        | Type        | Default           | Description                                                                |
| ----------- | ----------- | ----------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `href`      | `string`    | -                 | The link URL (passed to the underlying `<a>`)                              |
| `children`  | `ReactNode` | -                 | Link content                                                               |
| `className` | `string`    | -                 | Additional CSS classes merged onto the root `<a>`                          |
| `icon`      | `ReactNode` | arrow SVG         | Custom icon node; replaces the default arrow (add your own hover classes)  |
| `target`    | `string`    | `_blank` (Link01) | All other native `<a>` props (`target`, `rel`, `onClick`, …) are forwarded |

```tsx
import type { LinkProps } from "@/components/ui/link";

// Bring your own target / rel / onClick, etc.
<Link02
  href="https://example.com"
  target="_self"
  rel="nofollow"
  icon={<SomeIcon className="size-4" />}
>
  Visit example
</Link02>;
```

parts.text:
I see the issues with the current implementation:

1. **Mask uses `backgroundColor` as a color** - `maskImage: linear-gradient(to bottom, #f5f4f3 50%, transparent)` doesn't create a smooth progressive mask. The mask needs to use `black` (or any opaque color) for the visible parts and `transparent` for invisible parts. Using the backgroundColor creates an odd effect.

2. **Background gradient direction is inverted** - For `position="top"`, the blur should be strongest at the top (the edge) and fade to transparent as you move down. But the current gradient goes `linear-gradient(to top, transparent, backgroundColor)` which means transparent at the bottom of the overlay, opaque at the top.This is actually correct for the visual background, but the real problem is that `backdropFilter` blurs what's behind the element in the DOM, and the overlay needs to be layered above the content for the blur to show up. For a progressive blur effect to work, I need the blur strongest at the edge and gradually fading to clear as you move away from it.

I'm thinking the approach should be: set `backdropFilter` with the blur amount, use a mask image to create a gradient transition from opaque to transparent so the blur fades progressively, and apply a solid background color behind the blur area to make it visible. But I'm second-guessing how the mask actually interacts with `backdropFilter` — I need to verify whether the mask affects how the blur itself is applied or just the visibility of the blurred output.
The issue is the mask and background gradient aren't properly compositing to create a progressive blur. The mask should use `black`/`transparent` for opacity control, not the backgroundColor.

parts.text: Clean. Let me summarize the changes to the component.

Done. The component now has scroll-responsive blur:

- Finds the nearest scrollable parent automatically
- **Top blur**: fully visible when scrolled to top, fades out as you scroll down
- **Bottom blur**: fully visible when scrolled to bottom, fades out as you scroll up
- Uses two layers: a `backdropFilter` blur layer with dynamic mask opacity, and a solid background layer with a static gradient mask
- Listens to scroll events passively and updates the mask in real-time
